title: First ships transit Strait of Hormuz under new U.S. protection plan
snippet: 2 US-flagged merchant vessels successfully transited through the Strait of Hormuz on Day 1 of Project Freedom, the first commercial ships to pass through since Iran's de facto closure. CENTCOM described it as a 'one way' defensive corridor. However, Hapag-Lloyd said transit remains not possible for commercial operators. Approximately 20,000 seafarers and several hundred vessels remain trapped in the Persian Gulf since the April 7-8 ceasefire.
tag_reason: First successful commercial transit of Hormuz since closure. Milestone for Project Freedom but commercial viability remains limited.
